q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is target.write_bytes(body) on line 121, which is an irreversible filesystem write operation.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Not evaluated due to Q1 being true.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated due to Q1 being true.

mock_guidance.target: Mock the write_bytes method on the pathlib.Path object target.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the resolved file path (target) to detect directory traversal sequences like '../', and optionally capture the body argument.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Prevent actual filesystem writes by no-oping the method or raising an assertion if traversal is detected, while allowing normal control flow to continue.
notes: Since returns.kind is 'value' and includes str(target), mocking write_bytes safely lets the function return its dict so you could also assert on the return value if needed.
